name: Travelling Peddler
description: A lean figure in a patchwork cloak, surrounded by an eclectic assortment of goods spread on a blanket.

start.text: Welcome, welcome! Finest goods from across the realm. Trinkets, tonics, curiosities — what catches your eye?
start.narrative: The peddler spreads his arms wide over his wares.

responses.text: Where do you get your goods?
Any warnings about the road ahead?

source.text: Here and there. I travel the roads, trade with farmers, forage in the forest. Sometimes I find things... unusual things near the old ruins. Glowing stones, metal fragments that hum when you hold them.
source.narrative: He avoids your gaze while rearranging his wares.

stones.text: Found them at the forest edge, where the treeline meets the ruins. They were just... lying there, half-buried. I sold a few to the jeweler in town. He seemed very interested.

warnings.text: Don't go into the deep forest at night — wolves, and worse. The old farm's safe enough, and the ruins entrance is quiet by day. But the deeper you go toward the ruins hall... well, not everyone comes back.
warnings.narrative: He drops his salesman's cheer for a moment.

ruins.text: Bones, mostly. And old Eltharan stonework — beautiful, if you ignore the skeletal guardians. There's a hall deep inside with murals on the walls. And below that, the catacombs. I don't go that far.
